title: dryas drummondii f. tomentosa
intro: Dryas drummondii f. tomentosa, also known as tomentose mountain avens, is a subspecies of the Dryas drummondii plant, which is a flowering plant native to North America. The medicinal properties of tomentose mountain avens are attributed to its roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against infections. In herbal preparations, the roots of tomentose mountain avens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
